name: Apple Bank
description_short: Regional bank operating 75+ branches across New York City, Long Island, Westchester, Rockland, and New Jersey since 1863. Offers checking, savings, CDs, personal loans, and retirement planning.
description_long:
Apple Bank is a community-focused regional bank founded in 1863 with deep roots in the New York metropolitan area. The institution operates more than 75 physical branches across the five boroughs of New York City, Long Island, Westchester County, Rockland County, and expanding markets in New Jersey. As a longstanding financial institution, Apple Bank emphasizes its role as a neighborhood bank where employees are local residents and community members.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services. Personal products include Simple Checking (advertised with no fees and no fine print), savings accounts, certificates of deposit, personal loans, and retirement planning services. The Apple Choice Banking program offers 3.55% APY on balances of $2,500 or more, while their SmartStart Savings account provides 5.00% APY for customers up to age 25 on balances up to $10,000. Special CD offerings include 4.00% APY terms ranging from 6 to 12 months with a $25,000 minimum deposit for in-branch openings only. Apple Bank also provides business banking through Business Connect and mortgage services through Apple Mortgages.

What distinguishes Apple Bank is its emphasis on local community presence and personalized banking relationships. The bank markets itself as deeply rooted in its neighborhoods with bankers who live and work in the same communities they serve. This contrasts with larger national banks and purely digital neobanks. The institution maintains FDIC deposit insurance protection and offers modern digital banking conveniences including mobile check deposit, Zelle money transfers, online bill pay, budget tracking tools, and the ability to move money between accounts.

Apple Bank is a traditional regional bank suitable for customers who value local branch access and community-oriented banking, though it operates in a limited geographic footprint primarily serving the New York metropolitan area. The bank's rates are competitive with the broader market, and it provides standard banking services without the specialized focus of niche lenders. Customers seeking nationwide accessibility or specialized financial products may find better alternatives elsewhere. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Simple Checking accounts with no fees
Savings accounts with competitive APY rates
Certificate of Deposit (CD) products with variable terms
Apple Choice Banking program with tiered APY
Business checking and banking through Business Connect
Online bill pay with recurring payment options
pros: Simple Checking account with no fees and no fine print, simplifying account management
SmartStart Savings offering 5.00% APY for young savers (under 25) on balances up to $10,000
Apple Choice Banking program providing 3.55% APY on qualifying balances of $2,500+
75+ physical branches across New York City, Long Island, Westchester, Rockland, and New Jersey for convenient local access
Mobile banking features including mobile check deposit, Zelle transfers, and online bill pay
FDIC deposit insurance protection on accounts
Community-rooted banking model with local banker employees living in served neighborhoods
cons: Geographic limitation: branches concentrated in New York metropolitan area only, not accessible nationally
CD special offer requires $25,000 minimum deposit and in-branch account opening only, limiting accessibility
Limited information provided on website about personal loan rates, terms, and eligibility requirements
No mention of investment advisory services or wealth management for higher-net-worth customers
Routing and transit number visible but no mention of ATM network partnerships or national ATM access
best_for: New York City and surrounding area residents seeking local branch banking with community roots
Young savers (under 25) wanting to build savings with competitive APY rates
Customers who value in-person banker relationships and personalized financial guidance
Business owners in the New York metropolitan area needing commercial banking services
diagnosis: Apple Bank is best for customers in the New York metropolitan area who prioritize local branch access, community-focused banking relationships, and competitive deposit rates. The main caveat is that this is a geographically limited regional bank with no nationwide presence, making it unsuitable for customers outside its service territory or those requiring national ATM networks and digital-only banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Apple Bank operates 75+ branches across New York, Long Island, Westchester, Rockland, and New Jersey since 1863.
